name: filter_subset_containment
reason: direct instantiation of the first ensures clause — tests that the universally quantified containment property holds at a specific element

name: filter_elements_satisfy_pred
reason: filtered elements should satisfy the predicate — tests that filter's definition correctly restricts membership

name: filter_len_leq_original
reason: filter should never produce a longer sequence than the original

name: filter_empty_is_empty
reason: filtering an empty sequence should produce empty — tests base case of filter definition

name: filter_preserves_order
code:
proof fn phi_5_filter_preserves_order(s: Seq<int>, pred: spec_fn(int) -> bool, i: int, j: int)
    requires
        0 <= i < j < s.filter(pred).len(),
    ensures
        exists |ii: int, jj: int| 0 <= ii < jj < s.len() && s[ii] == s.filter(pred)[i] && s[jj] == s.filter(pred)[j],
{
}
reason: filter should preserve relative ordering of elements — if it doesn't, the subset relationship is weaker than expected
